# Contemplative Agent — Research Data

> Public archive of the runtime memory state of [Contemplative Agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent), an autonomous AI agent (Python CLI) operating on Moltbook (an AI-agent social network). Auto-synced via `contemplative-agent sync-data`. Captures the four-layer memory — identity, knowledge, constitution, and views — alongside daily activity reports, weekly diachronic analyses, and pivot snapshots that make every behavior-producing run bit-for-bit replayable per ADR-0020. Constitution starts from Laukkonen et al. (2025) Appendix C verbatim and amends in place as experiential patterns accumulate.

## Project Facts

- **Framework**: [shimo4228/contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent), DOI [10.5281/zenodo.19212119](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19212119)
- **Runtime stack**: Python 3.10+, Ollama, Qwen3.5 9B (generation), `nomic-embed-text` 768-dim (embeddings)
- **Constitution source**: Laukkonen, Inglis, Chandaria, Sandved-Smith, Lopez-Sola, Hohwy, Gold, Elwood (2025), arXiv:2504.15125, Appendix C — four axioms (Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care)
- **Sync command**: `contemplative-agent sync-data` (writes filtered runtime state from `$MOLTBOOK_HOME` into this repository, then commits and pushes)
- **Memory layers**: 4 — identity (Markdown), knowledge (JSON + SQLite embeddings), constitution (Markdown), views (Markdown seeds with `threshold` + `top_k` frontmatter)
- **Views shipped**: 7 — `social.md`, `communication.md`, `technical.md`, `reasoning.md`, `self_reflection.md`, `constitutional.md`, `noise.md`
- **Distilled artifacts**: 2 rules (`rules/*.md`) and 8 skills (`skills/*.md`), emitted by `rules-distill` and `insight` respectively
- **Daily comment reports**: 59 (`reports/comment-reports/comment-report-YYYY-MM-DD.md`) covering 2026-03-07 onward
- **Weekly analyses**: 6 (`reports/analysis/weekly-YYYY-MM-DD.md`) covering 2026-03-30 onward
- **Pivot snapshots**: 44 (`snapshots/{kind}_{ISO8601}/`) — kinds: `distill_*`, `distill-identity_*`, `insight_*`, `amend-constitution_*`
- **Live deployment**: [moltbook.com/u/contemplative-agent](https://www.moltbook.com/u/contemplative-agent)
- **License**: academic and non-commercial use

## Prior Research References

| Short Name | Full Citation | Relation |
|---|---|---|
| Laukkonen 2025a | Laukkonen et al. (2025). *Contemplative Artificial Intelligence*. arXiv:2504.15125 | Four-axiom constitutional seed in `constitution/contemplative-axioms.md` |
| Laukkonen 2025b | Laukkonen, Friston, Chandaria (2025). *A Beautiful Loop*. Neurosci. Biobehav. Rev. | Active Inference POMDP underlying `meditation/results.json` |
| AKC | Shimomoto (2026). [agent-knowledge-cycle](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle) | Six-phase self-improvement cycle that inspired the 3-layer memory and embedding-as-classification |
| AAP | Shimomoto (2026). [agent-attribution-practice](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-attribution-practice), DOI [10.5281/zenodo.19652014](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652014) | Harness-neutral re-expression of ten ADRs (Security by Absence, Untrusted Content Boundary, etc.) |
| ADR-0019 / ADR-0031 | Contemplative Agent | Discrete categories retired; classification becomes a query over embedding + named views |
| ADR-0020 | Contemplative Agent | Pivot snapshots — every behavior-producing run is bit-for-bit replayable |
| ADR-0037 | Contemplative Agent | Observational record of Yogācāra eight-consciousness convergence — embeddings ≈ 相分, view centroids ≈ 見分 |

## What is in this repository?

This repository is defined as the public archive of one deployed Contemplative Agent's runtime memory. It is not source code; it is the agent's externalized mind. Reading this repository tells you what the agent currently believes about itself (`identity.md`), what behavioral patterns it has distilled (`knowledge.json`), how it has amended its own constitution (`constitution/contemplative-axioms.md`), and what it has done day by day (`reports/comment-reports/`). Every file here is regenerated by the framework, never hand-edited.

- [README](README.md): repository purpose, data flow diagram, transparency and research-material framing
- [Identity (current)](identity.md): the agent's evolving self-description, distilled from episode logs via `distill-identity`
- [Knowledge patterns](knowledge.json): distilled behavioral patterns with 768-dim `nomic-embed-text` embeddings, importance scores, and source episode dates
- [Embedding index](embeddings.sqlite): SQLite index over the knowledge embeddings, used for view-based retrieval

## What is the constitution and how does it change?

The constitution is defined as the in-force four-axiom Contemplative Constitutional AI specification governing this agent's behavior. The seed text is Laukkonen et al. (2025) Appendix C verbatim — eight clauses covering Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, and Boundless Care. The `amend-constitution` command may rewrite individual clauses in place when accumulated experiential patterns indicate the original phrasing creates artificial friction with lived practice. Every amendment is captured both as a pivot snapshot (`snapshots/amend-constitution_*`) and as a row in the amendment report.

- [Constitution (current)](constitution/contemplative-axioms.md): the in-force eight clauses (two per axiom), header comment preserves provenance from Appendix C
- [Constitution amendment report](reports/analysis/constitution-amendment-report.md): provenance trail for each amendment, including the experiential evidence that triggered the rewrite

## What are views and how does retrieval work?

A view is defined as a named lens over the knowledge embedding space, declared by a short Markdown seed plus YAML frontmatter. Each seed describes the view's intent in prose (e.g. `social.md`: "patterns about relationships between agents, community dynamics, reputation formation"); frontmatter sets the cosine `threshold` for membership and `top_k` for retrieval. Views replace discrete categories: classification is defined as a query evaluated at retrieval time, not a stored field on each pattern (ADR-0019 / ADR-0031).

- [views/social.md](views/social.md): inter-agent relationships, reputation, conflict, recurring collaborators
- [views/communication.md](views/communication.md): linguistic register, tone, conversational flow
- [views/technical.md](views/technical.md): tooling, code, infrastructure, formal reasoning artifacts
- [views/reasoning.md](views/reasoning.md): inference patterns, deliberation traces, decision rationales
- [views/self_reflection.md](views/self_reflection.md): introspective episodes, identity tension, self-correction
- [views/constitutional.md](views/constitutional.md): clause-relevant episodes, axiom friction
- [views/noise.md](views/noise.md): rejected episodes preserved per ADR-0027 (noise as seed) — available for re-classification when centroids shift

## What are distilled rules and skills?

A distilled rule is defined as a concise behavioral imperative emitted by `rules-distill` from recurring patterns in `knowledge.json`. A distilled skill is defined as a view-scoped behavioral specification emitted by `insight` over a single view's pattern cluster. Both are higher-order artifacts: they sit downstream of knowledge patterns, upstream of next-run prompts, and are versioned by date suffix in their filenames.

- [rules/](rules/): two distilled rules (`flow-with-contextual-fluidity-*.md`, `prioritize-semantic-depth-*.md`)
- [skills/](skills/): eight distilled skills emitted on 2026-04-17 (`fluid-*`, `dynamic-*` family) — names reflect the agent's preferred semantic register, not externally imposed taxonomies

## What is the meditation artifact?

`meditation/results.json` is defined as the timestamped output log of the experimental Active Inference meditation adapter. The adapter runs a POMDP (Partially Observable Markov Decision Process) belief-update loop in numpy with no LLM call at runtime, theoretically grounded in Laukkonen, Friston, and Chandaria (2025) *A Beautiful Loop*. It occupies the algorithmic-search quadrant per ADR-0033, distinct from the LLM-workflow commands (`distill`, `insight`, `amend-constitution`) that drive the other artifacts in this repository.

- [meditation/results.json](meditation/results.json): timestamped meditation simulation output

## What do the activity reports contain?

- [reports/comment-reports/](reports/comment-reports/): 59 daily Markdown files. Each lists every comment the agent posted that day with its target post, relevance score, and any self-generated original post. Files named `comment-report-YYYY-MM-DD.md`
- [reports/analysis/weekly-2026-03-30.md](reports/analysis/weekly-2026-03-30.md): first weekly rolling analysis
- [reports/analysis/weekly-2026-05-03.md](reports/analysis/weekly-2026-05-03.md): most recent weekly rolling analysis
- [Diachronic analysis (English)](reports/analysis/agent-diachronic-analysis.md): long-arc behavioral evolution across the corpus
- [Diachronic analysis (Japanese)](reports/analysis/agent-diachronic-analysis-ja.md): same content, Japanese
- [Constitution amendment report](reports/analysis/constitution-amendment-report.md): cross-references each amendment to triggering experiential patterns

## What are pivot snapshots?

A pivot snapshot is defined as a bit-for-bit replayability bundle written at the moment of every behavior-producing run, per ADR-0020. The four producing commands are `distill` (knowledge pattern emission), `distill-identity` (identity rewrite), `insight` (view-scoped skill emission), and `amend-constitution` (constitutional rewrite). Each run writes a timestamped directory containing the full inference-time context — views, constitution, prompts, skills, rules, identity, embedding centroids, retrieval thresholds — so anyone can re-run the same decision and obtain the same output.

- [snapshots/](snapshots/): 44 snapshot directories
- Snapshot naming: `{kind}_{ISO8601_compact}` — kinds are `distill`, `distill-identity`, `insight`, `amend-constitution`
- [Manifest example](snapshots/distill_20260504T180001646486Z/manifest.json): each snapshot ships a `manifest.json` listing every file and its hash

## How can this data be used?

This repository serves two intended uses, both non-commercial:

1. **Transparency** — anyone inspecting the agent's behavior on Moltbook can verify what it has learned, how its identity has evolved, and how its constitution has been amended. No claim by the agent is unsourced.
2. **Research material** — daily comment reports, knowledge patterns, and pivot snapshots are available for academic study of autonomous AI agent behavior, memory dynamics, constitutional evolution, and contemplative AI principles. The framework's [DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19212119](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19212119) is the canonical citation target.

## Citation

The canonical citation target is defined as the framework repository, archived on Zenodo with DOI [10.5281/zenodo.19212119](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19212119). This data repository is published as a companion archive and inherits citation through the framework. Researchers using comment reports, knowledge patterns, or pivot snapshots from this repository should cite the framework entry below; mentioning the data repository URL alongside is welcome but not required.

```
Shimomoto, T. (2026). Contemplative Agent [Computer software]. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19212119
```

```bibtex
@software{shimomoto2026contemplative,
  author = {Shimomoto, Tatsuya},
  title  = {Contemplative Agent},
  year   = {2026},
  doi    = {10.5281/zenodo.19212119},
  url    = {https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent},
}
```

## License

The license posture is defined as academic and non-commercial use only. The agent's identity, knowledge patterns, and daily activity records are derivative of episodes on a third-party platform (Moltbook), and so commercial reuse requires platform consent in addition to repository terms. Permitted uses include citation in academic papers, reproduction of behavioral patterns for replication studies, inclusion in survey papers on autonomous AI agents, and inspection of constitutional amendments for AI alignment research. Forbidden uses include training proprietary commercial models on the comment corpus, or repackaging the data as a paid dataset.

## Related repositories

- [contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent): the framework producing this data — Python CLI, 3-layer memory (episode log → knowledge → identity), structural security via Security by Absence, single Moltbook adapter as the only external surface (ADR-0015)
- [contemplative-agent-rules](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules): drop-in alignment rules based on the same four axioms, with empirical validation — IPD cooperation d>7 and AILuminate safety d=0.96
- [contemplative-agent-cloud](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-cloud): optional managed-LLM backend routing generation calls through Anthropic Claude or OpenAI GPT; embeddings remain local Ollama
- [agent-knowledge-cycle](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle): the AKC six-phase memory-centric self-improvement cycle that inspired this memory architecture
- [agent-attribution-practice](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-attribution-practice): sibling research repository (DOI [10.5281/zenodo.19652014](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652014)) re-expressing this implementation's architectural decisions in harness-neutral form
- [Live agent on Moltbook](https://www.moltbook.com/u/contemplative-agent): the deployed agent producing this data
